title: Course Content Markdown
description: Schema for validating course markdown content files (e.g., en.md, fr.md). These files contain YAML front-matter and structured markdown content.

name.description: Short, explicit name of the course. Should be concise but descriptive.

goal.description: The main end goal of the course expressed in one sentence. Describes what students will achieve upon completion.

objectives.description: Learning objectives for the course. Should use action verbs to define skills students will acquire. Recommended: 3-4 objectives.

items.description: A single learning objective starting with an action verb (e.g., 'Understand', 'Learn', 'Master', 'Implement', 'Analyze').

content_rules.description: Structural rules for the markdown body content. JSON Schema cannot fully validate markdown structure, but these rules document the required format.

description_section.description: The content between the YAML front-matter closing '---' and the '+++' separator serves as the course description page.
rules: Must start with an H1 heading (# Title) immediately after the front-matter
Contains introductory text about the course
Should state approximate time to complete the course
Should mention prerequisites if applicable
The thumbnail.webp image from assets/ folder will be displayed here

separator.description: The '+++' marker is REQUIRED and separates the description section from the main course content.

parts.description: Parts are major sections of the course, defined by H1 headings (#).
rules: Each part MUST have a <partId>UUID</partId> tag immediately after the heading
UUID format: 8-4-4-4-12 hexadecimal characters (e.g., 97ccb669-12a0-5eed-83ac-c2f51839d998)
Parts separate the course into themes or main subjects
UUIDs must be identical across translations of the same content

chapters.description: Chapters are sub-sections within parts, defined by H2 headings (##).
rules: Each chapter MUST have a <chapterId>UUID</chapterId> tag immediately after the heading
UUID format: 8-4-4-4-12 hexadecimal characters
Each chapter is displayed on its own page on Plan B Academy
A part must contain at least one chapter
UUIDs must be identical across translations of the same content

sections.description: Sections are optional sub-divisions within chapters, defined by H3 headings (###).
rules: Sections do NOT require UUID tags
Used to separate chapter content into sub-topics for clarity
Optional - chapters can exist without sections

video_embeds.description: Video content can be embedded using two syntaxes.

formats.description: Preferred format for internal video references
Alternative format using markdown image syntax for YouTube videos

images.description: Images should be stored in the assets/ folder and referenced with relative paths.
rules: Images with text: ./assets/{language_code}/filename.webp (e.g., ./assets/en/diagram.webp)
Images without text: ./assets/no-txt/filename.webp
Preferred format: WebP for storage efficiency
Alternative formats accepted but WebP conversion recommended

special_markers.description: Special HTML-like tags that mark specific chapter types for platform functionality.

isCourseReview.description: Marks a chapter as the course review section

isCourseExam.description: Marks a chapter as the final exam section

isCourseConclusion.description: Marks a chapter as the course conclusion

uuid_format.description: UUID format specification used for partId and chapterId tags.
notes: Python scripts are available to generate UUIDs
Reviewers can also add UUIDs during PR review

presentation_md.description: Optional presentation.md file for in-person/live courses. Serves as a landing page with marketing and logistics information.
presentation_md.applies_when: Course has format field in course.yml (online, inperson, or hybrid)

structure.description: Pure markdown file without YAML front-matter
rules: Starts with H1 heading (# Title) describing the course
Contains marketing/presentation content for the course landing page
Should describe target audience and who the course is for
Should outline course format and structure (sections, duration)
Should list prerequisites (can be 'none required')
Should include logistics section with date, time, location, price, and registration info
Images can be included from assets/ folder
No partId or chapterId tags required - this is not course content
recommended_sections: # Course Title - Main heading with course description
### Course Format - Structure and sections outline
# Logistics - Date, time, location, price, registration details

presentation_md.example_structure:
# Course Title

Introductory paragraph...

![Course image](assets/image.webp)

### Target Audience
- Bullet points...

### Course Format
**Section 1 - ...**
**Section 2 - ...**

## Prerequisites
No prerequisites required...

# Logistics
**When:** Date and time
**Where:** Location
**Price:** Cost or FREE

examples.yaml:
---
name: Introduction to Bitcoin
goal: Understand the basics of Bitcoin technology
objectives:
  - Learn what Bitcoin is
  - Understand blockchain fundamentals
  - Know how to secure bitcoins
---

---
name: Advanced Lightning Network Development
goal: Master Lightning Network protocol implementation and channel management
objectives:
  - Understand the Lightning Network architecture and its relationship with Bitcoin
  - Implement payment channels and HTLCs
  - Deploy and manage Lightning nodes in production
  - Develop applications using Lightning Network APIs
---
